title: json_extract_string_field silently accepts trailing garbage after target field
description: What is awkward: the function validates JSON strictly before the target field (control chars, escape sequences, surrogate pairs) but returns immediately on match without validating trailing content. Input like {"key":"val" GARBAGE} returns Ok with 'val'. This creates an asymmetry: a document invalid because of trailing garbage succeeds if the target field comes first, but fails if it comes second. Suggested rewrite: after extracting the value, continue the normal separator/close loop to validate remaining structure, or add a clear comment that this function is an extractor (not a validator) and that post-extraction content is intentionally not checked.
